4. Overlays multiple fly trajectories for the same condition using `plot_traj_xflies`
5. Visualizes pre-stimulus, stimulus, and post-stimulus trajectory segments using `plot_traj_pre_post`

All trajectory plots share a common coordinate system (arena center at approximately 123 mm, 125 mm) and plot the stimulus period (frames 300--1200, i.e., 30 seconds at 30 fps) by default.

**Key functions introduced:**

### \colorbox{fnbg}{\texttt{plot\_traj\_subplot.m}} \label{fn:plot_traj_subplot}

Generates a grid of subplots showing each fly's trajectory in the arena during the stimulus period for one condition. Up to 35 flies per figure; additional figures are created automatically if needed.

```matlab
plot_traj_subplot(DATA, strain, condition_n, ...
    save_folder)
```

![Example trajectory subplot grid: 2D paths of individual empty-split control flies during condition 1 (60-deg gratings at 4 Hz). Each subplot shows one fly's path during the 30-second stimulus.](example_figs/ex_trajectory_subplot.png){#fig-ex-traj-subplot width=90%}

### \colorbox{fnbg}{\texttt{plot\_traj\_xcond.m}} \label{fn:plot_traj_xcond}

Overlays a single fly's trajectory across multiple stimulus conditions on the same axes. Each condition is rendered in a different color, revealing how the fly's movement pattern changes between stimuli.

```matlab
plot_traj_xcond(DATA, strain, cond_ids, fly_idx)
```

![Example cross-condition trajectory: one fly's movement path during static gratings (cond.~10), flicker (cond.~9), and 4 Hz gratings (cond.~1). Different conditions produce visually distinct walking patterns.](example_figs/ex_trajectory_xcond.png){#fig-ex-traj-xcond width=50%}

### \colorbox{fnbg}{\texttt{plot\_traj\_xflies.m}} \label{fn:plot_traj_xflies}

Overlays trajectories from multiple flies for a single stimulus condition on the same axes. Useful for showing variability between individuals.

```matlab
plot_traj_xflies(DATA, strain, cond_idx, fly_ids)
```

![Example multi-fly trajectory: three empty-split control flies during condition 1 (4 Hz gratings), overlaid on the same arena plot. Each fly's path during the 30-second stimulus is shown.](example_figs/ex_trajectory_xflies.png){#fig-ex-traj-xflies width=50%}

### \colorbox{fnbg}{\texttt{plot\_traj\_pre\_post.m}} \label{fn:plot_traj_pre_post}

Visualizes trajectory segments before, during, and after stimulus onset for one or more flies. Pre-stimulus and post-stimulus segments (5 seconds each) are drawn in grey; the stimulus period is drawn in the condition color. This makes stimulus-evoked changes in locomotion visually obvious.

```matlab
plot_traj_pre_post(DATA, strain, cond_idx, ...
    fly_ids)
```

![Example pre/post trajectory: one fly's path before stimulus (grey), during 4 Hz gratings (colored), and after stimulus (grey). The change in walking pattern at stimulus onset is visible.](example_figs/ex_trajectory_prepost.png){#fig-ex-traj-prepost width=50%}


\newpage

# Utility Functions {#sec-utilities}

## \colorbox{fnbg}{\texttt{check\_strain\_typos.m}} \label{fn:check_strain_typos}

Corrects common strain name typos that may have been entered through the metadata GUI. Maps misspelled variants to canonical strain names, ensuring consistent naming across the dataset.

## \colorbox{fnbg}{\texttt{generate\_circ\_stim\_ufmf.m}} \label{fn:generate_circ_stim_ufmf}

Generates a UFMF video of the LED stimulus pattern (circular projection) for documentation and verification purposes. Creates a video showing what the flies see from above.


\newpage

# Potential Pitfalls {#sec-pitfalls}

When adapting this pipeline to a new experimental setup, protocol, or fly strain, be aware of the following assumptions that are hardcoded in the current codebase. Each can cause silent errors if not addressed.

## Hardcoded Arena Parameters

`calculate_viewing_distance.m` contains hardcoded calibration constants:

```matlab
PPM = 4.1691;          % pixels per mm
CoA = [528, 520]/PPM;  % arena center in mm
R   = 496/PPM;         % arena radius in mm
```

These values are specific to the current camera and arena setup. If the camera, lens, or arena changes, these must be recalibrated. Incorrect values will silently corrupt all viewing distance and distance-from-center metrics.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/calculate_viewing_distance.m`, lines 39--43.

## Hardcoded Project Paths

`process_freely_walking_data.m` sets an absolute project root:

```matlab
PROJECT_ROOT = '\Users\burnettl\...';
```

This path must be updated for any other machine before running the processing pipeline.

**Source:** `processing_functions/process_freely_walking_data.m`, line 42.

## Protocol Condition Arrays Are Per-Protocol

`comb_data_one_cohort_cond.m` contains a large `if`/`elseif` block that maps each protocol name to its condition array. New protocols must either:

(a) Store conditions in `LOG.meta.cond_array` (as `protocol_24` and `protocol_27` already do --- this is the recommended approach), or

(b) Add a new `elseif` branch to this function.

If neither is done, condition matching will silently fail and produce incorrect results.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/comb_data_one_cohort_cond.m`, lines 3--126.

## Exactly 2 Repetitions Assumed

`comb_data_one_cohort_cond.m` splits the `log_` fields into the first half (Repetition 1) and second half (Repetition 2):

```matlab
if log_n < (n_cond/2) + 1
    rep_str = 'R1_condition_';
else
    rep_str = 'R2_condition_';
end
```

Running 3 or more repetitions, or only 1, will mislabel conditions. The code does not verify that the number of logged conditions equals exactly 2 times the number of unique conditions.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/comb_data_one_cohort_cond.m`, line 203.

## Velocity Threshold of 50 mm/s

`combine_data_one_cohort.m` flags frames where velocity exceeds 50 mm/s as tracking errors and sets them to NaN:

```matlab
vals_to_rm = abs(vel_data(k, :)) > 50;
```

This threshold is appropriate for *Drosophila melanogaster* but may need adjustment for larger or faster species. Real high-speed behaviour is indistinguishable from tracking errors at this threshold.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/combine_data_one_cohort.m`, lines 66 and 130.

## 300-Frame Pre-Buffer Is Hardcoded

`comb_data_one_cohort_cond.m` always includes 300 frames (10 seconds at 30 fps) before each condition onset:

```matlab
framesb4 = 300;
start_f = Log.start_f(1) - framesb4;
```

If a stimulus starts fewer than 300 frames after acclimation ends, this can produce out-of-bounds frame indices.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/comb_data_one_cohort_cond.m`, line 228.

## LOG Field Naming Is Fragile

Condition discovery searches for fields whose names start with `log_`:

```matlab
logfields = fields(startsWith(fields, 'log_'));
```

Any extra field added to the LOG struct with a name beginning with `log_` (e.g., `log_notes`, `log_metadata`) will be incorrectly treated as a stimulus condition.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/comb_data_one_cohort_cond.m`, line 195.

## Pattern-to-Description Mapping

`get_title_from_meta.m` uses a large switch statement to map pattern IDs to human-readable descriptions. New patterns require adding a manual entry. Additionally, some patterns have hidden speed multipliers --- patterns with 2-pixel animation steps (e.g., patterns 26, 27, 63) automatically double the nominal speed value, which is easy to overlook.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/get_title_from_meta.m`.

## Tracking QC Uses Statistical Mode

`check_tracking_FlyTrk.m` removes any fly whose frame count differs from the mode (most common frame count):

```matlab
d = [trx.nframes];
nframes_video = mode(d);
```

If a systematic tracking issue causes all flies to be off by one frame, no flies will be flagged. Conversely, if tracking splits are common, valid flies may be removed because their frame count differs from the mode.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/check_tracking_FlyTrk.m`.

## Strain Typos Pass Silently

`check_strain_typos.m` corrects only a predefined list of known typos. New misspellings are not caught and will create separate strain folders in the results directory, splitting data for the same genotype across multiple entries. Always verify strain names after data collection.

**Source:** `processing_functions/functions/check_strain_typos.m`.

## Move-Folder Nesting Bug in Daily Processing

In `daily_processing.py`, the `move_folder()` function uses `shutil.move(src, dst)`. If the destination folder already exists, `shutil.move` places the source folder *inside* the destination, creating a nested structure (e.g., `2_processed/2025_04_18/2025_04_18/...`). The function includes a warning comment but does not prevent this.

By contrast, `reprocessing_script.py`'s `move_folder()` deletes the destination first with `shutil.rmtree(dst)` before moving -- which avoids nesting but is destructive.

**Source:** `daily_processing/daily_processing.py`, lines 161--183; `daily_processing/reprocessing_script.py`, lines 105--119.

## No Retry Logic for Network File Operations

All automation scripts log network copy/move failures but do not retry them. A transient network outage during `shutil.copytree()` or `shutil.move()` will leave data in an intermediate state (partially copied, or stuck in the source directory). Manual intervention is required to re-run or complete the transfer.

**Source:** All three main automation scripts (`monitor_and_copy.py`, `monitor_and_track.py`, `daily_processing.py`).

## Duplicated Path Constants Across Automation Scripts

Each automation script defines its own copy of network and local path constants. There is no shared configuration file. If a path changes (e.g., the network share is moved), every script must be updated independently. Currently, `daily_processing.py` and `reprocessing_script.py` contain nearly identical path blocks.

**Source:** Path constants at the top of `monitor_and_copy.py`, `monitor_and_track.py`, `daily_processing.py`, and `reprocessing_script.py`.


\newpage

\appendix

# Function Index {#sec-index}

Alphabetical listing of all MATLAB functions referenced in this guide. Page numbers indicate the primary documentation location.

\begin{scriptsize}
\begin{tabularx}{\textwidth}{>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{5.5cm} c >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}X}
\textbf{Function} & \textbf{Page} & \textbf{Description} \\
\hline
\texttt{batch\_track\_ufmf} & \pageref{fn:batch_track_ufmf} & Batch FlyTracker processing for all videos in a folder \\
\texttt{calculate\_distance\_to\_\newline nearest\_fly} & \pageref{fn:calculate_distance_to_nearest_fly} & Compute IFD and IFA for each fly \\
\texttt{calculate\_three\_point\_velocity} & \pageref{fn:calculate_three_point_velocity} & Central-difference speed estimate \\
\texttt{calculate\_viewing\_distance} & \pageref{fn:calculate_viewing_distance} & Ray-circle intersection for viewing distance \\
\texttt{check\_strain\_typos} & \pageref{fn:check_strain_typos} & Correct common strain name misspellings \\
\texttt{check\_tracking\_FlyTrk} & \pageref{fn:check_tracking_FlyTrk} & QC: remove bad fly tracks by frame count \\
\texttt{comb\_data\_across\_cohorts\_cond} & \pageref{fn:comb_data_across_cohorts_cond} & Merge experiments into hierarchical DATA struct \\
\texttt{comb\_data\_one\_cohort\_cond} & \pageref{fn:comb_data_one_cohort_cond} & Split continuous data by condition using LOG \\
\texttt{combine\_data\_one\_cohort} & \pageref{fn:combine_data_one_cohort} & Core: compute 12 behavioral metrics \\
\texttt{export\_to\_google\_sheets} & \pageref{fn:export_to_google_sheets} & Push metadata to Google Sheet via HTTP \\
\texttt{fig\_different\_speeds} & \pageref{fn:fig_different_speeds} & Speed comparison analysis (protocol\_31) \\
\texttt{fig1\_plots} & \pageref{fn:fig1_plots} & Publication figures for MIC paper (protocol\_27) \\
\texttt{gaussian\_conv} & \pageref{fn:gaussian_conv} & Gaussian smoothing for position data \\
\texttt{generate\_circ\_stim\_ufmf} & \pageref{fn:generate_circ_stim_ufmf} & Generate stimulus video for documentation \\
\texttt{generate\_trajectory\_plots} & \pageref{fn:generate_trajectory_plots} & Trajectory visualization script \\
\texttt{get\_input\_parameters} & \pageref{fn:get_input_parameters} & GUI dialog for experiment metadata \\
\texttt{get\_temp\_rec} & \pageref{fn:get_temp_rec} & Read temperature from DAQ probes \\
\texttt{initialize\_temp\_recording} & \pageref{fn:initialize_temp_recording} & Configure DAQ for temperature monitoring \\
\texttt{initialize\_video\_and\_folders} & \pageref{fn:initialize_video_and_folders} & Camera setup, folder creation, LOG init \\
\texttt{make\_overview} & \pageref{fn:make_overview} & Summary histograms for QC \\
\texttt{make\_summary\_heat\_maps\_p27} & \pageref{fn:make_summary_heat_maps_p27} & P-value heatmap across strains \\
\texttt{plot\_all\_features\_acclim} & \pageref{fn:plot_all_features_acclim} & Acclimation period timeseries plots \\
\texttt{plot\_all\_features\_filt} & \pageref{fn:plot_all_features_filt} & Full-experiment timeseries plots \\
\texttt{plot\_allcond\_acrossgroups\_\newline tuning} & \pageref{fn:plot_allcond_acrossgroups_tuning} & Strain vs control timeseries overlay \\
\texttt{plot\_allcond\_onecohort\_tuning} & \pageref{fn:plot_allcond_onecohort_tuning} & Per-condition comparison plots \\
\texttt{plot\_boxchart\_metrics\_xcond} & \pageref{fn:plot_boxchart_metrics_xcond} & Box plots comparing conditions \\
\texttt{plot\_boxchart\_metrics\_xstrains} & \pageref{fn:plot_boxchart_metrics_xstrains} & Box plots comparing strains \\
\texttt{plot\_fly\_occupancy\_\newline heatmaps\_all} & \pageref{fn:plot_fly_occupancy_heatmaps_all} & Spatial occupancy heatmaps (pooled) \\
\texttt{plot\_traj\_pre\_post} & \pageref{fn:plot_traj_pre_post} & Pre/stimulus/post trajectory segments \\
\texttt{plot\_traj\_subplot} & \pageref{fn:plot_traj_subplot} & Trajectory subplot grid (all flies) \\
\texttt{plot\_traj\_xcond} & \pageref{fn:plot_traj_xcond} & Single-fly trajectory across conditions \\
\texttt{plot\_traj\_xflies} & \pageref{fn:plot_traj_xflies} & Multi-fly trajectory for one condition \\
\texttt{plot\_xcond\_per\_strain2} & \pageref{fn:plot_xcond_per_strain2} & Cross-condition timeseries for one strain \\
\texttt{plot\_xstrain\_per\_cond} & \pageref{fn:plot_xstrain_per_cond} & Cross-strain timeseries for one condition \\
\texttt{present\_acclim\_off} & \pageref{fn:present_acclim_off} & Dark acclimation recording \\
\texttt{present\_fixation\_stimulus} & \pageref{fn:present_fixation_stimulus} & Static bar display for fixation \\
\texttt{present\_optomotor\_stimulus} & \pageref{fn:present_optomotor_stimulus} & Standard bidirectional stimulus \\
\texttt{present\_optomotor\_stimulus\_\newline curtain} & \pageref{fn:present_optomotor_stimulus_curtain} & Curtain pattern stimulus \\
\texttt{present\_optomotor\_stimulus\_\newline curtain\_w\_interval} & \pageref{fn:present_optomotor_stimulus_curtain_w_interval} & Curtain with per-trial intervals \\
\texttt{present\_optomotor\_stimulus\_\newline diff\_contrasts} & \pageref{fn:present_optomotor_stimulus_diff_contrasts} & Contrast variation stimulus \\
\texttt{present\_optomotor\_stimulus\_\newline one\_direction} & \pageref{fn:present_optomotor_stimulus_one_direction} & Single-direction stimulus \\
\texttt{present\_optomotor\_stimulus\_\newline w\_interval} & \pageref{fn:present_optomotor_stimulus_w_interval} & Stimulus with per-trial intervals \\
\texttt{present\_shifted\_stimulus} & \pageref{fn:present_shifted_stimulus} & Open-loop shifted patterns \\
\texttt{process\_data\_features} & \pageref{fn:process_data_features} & Single-experiment processing \\
\texttt{process\_freely\_walking\_data} & \pageref{fn:process_freely_walking_data} & Top-level processing by date \\
\texttt{vel\_estimate} & \pageref{fn:vel_estimate} & Sliding-window angular velocity \\
\hline
\\
\textbf{Automation Scripts (Python)} & & \\
\hline
\texttt{monitor\_and\_copy.py} & \pageref{fn:monitor_and_copy} & Watchdog-based copy from acquisition PC to network \\
\texttt{monitor\_and\_track.py} & \pageref{fn:monitor_and_track} & Polling-based automated FlyTracker batch tracking \\
\texttt{daily\_processing.py} & \pageref{fn:daily_processing} & Daily MATLAB processing and result distribution \\
\end{tabularx}
\end{scriptsize}
